user_query:
I coordinate our youth ministry and want a weekly automated attendance report I can prep on Saturday evenings for Sunday morning. Please implement a small, self-contained job that reads the provided data, computes the core stats, and schedules itself to run weekly. Use these inputs and paths:

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/attendance_log.csv (attendance records; schema: date,group_id,activity,headcount)
- input/roster.json (valid groups with names and leaders)
- config/schedule.yaml (job config you must update)

What to do:
1) Modify config/schedule.yaml to schedule the job for Saturdays at 18:00 local time (update schedule.day_of_week and schedule.time only; keep other fields as-is).
2) Create a script at tools/generate_reports.py that:
   - Reads config/schedule.yaml.
   - Loads input/attendance_log.csv and input/roster.json.
   - Determines the report date as the maximum date present in input/attendance_log.csv.
   - Computes for the report date:
     - Overall headcount (sum of headcount over all records for that date).
     - Per-group headcount for roster groups on that date (0 if the group has no records that date).
     - Top 3 activities by headcount on that date.
     - Week-over-week percent change per roster group compared to the most recent previous date in the CSV (null if no previous record for that group).
     - Data checks: list of records whose group_id is not in the roster (unknown_groups), and list of roster group_ids missing any record on the report date (missing_groups).
   - Also computes a rolling 4-date window summary over the latest up to 4 unique dates in the CSV: per roster group, total_headcount and avg_headcount across those dates, plus the number of dates included (weeks_counted).
   - Writes structured outputs to local files as below.
3) Write the outputs to these exact paths and structures:
   - reports/weekly/<report_date>/summary.json with fields: {report_date: YYYY-MM-DD, generated_at: ISO timestamp, totals: {overall_headcount: number}, groups: [ {group_id, group_name, leader, headcount, week_over_week_change_pct|null} ], top_activities: [ {activity, headcount} ] (up to 3), data_checks: {unknown_groups: [ {date, group_id, activity, headcount} ], missing_groups: [group_id, ...] }.
   - reports/weekly/<report_date>/group_stats.csv with columns: group_id, group_name, leader, headcount.
   - reports/rolling_4_weeks.csv with columns: group_id, group_name, leader, weeks_counted, total_headcount, avg_headcount.
   Use the max date in the CSV as <report_date> (with the provided sample, it should be 2024-09-28).
4) Generate a cron manifest at ops/weekly_reports.cron containing one line that schedules the job every Saturday at 18:00 local time and runs: python tools/generate_reports.py --config config/schedule.yaml. The cron schedule must match the updated schedule in config/schedule.yaml.
5) Perform one run now using the current inputs so the output files are created under reports/ and ops/weekly_reports.cron is written. Do not rely on any external services.

Acceptance notes:
- The modified config/schedule.yaml must show day_of_week: Saturday and time: "18:00".
- The reports/weekly/2024-09-28/ folder must exist with summary.json and group_stats.csv populated as specified.
- reports/rolling_4_weeks.csv must reflect aggregates over the latest up to 4 unique dates in input/attendance_log.csv.
- ops/weekly_reports.cron must contain exactly one line at Saturday 18:00 that invokes the script with the provided config path. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
